channel.title: Research on z-r0crypt
channel.description: Recent content in Research on z-r0crypt

item.title: From OSCP to OSEP: Does it Fill the Red Team Gap?
item.description: What OSCP doesn't teach you and why it matters for real enterprise work. A practitioner's perspective on the gap between entry-level certification and red team readiness, written after passing OSCP in 2017 and OSEP while actively working in red teaming.

item.title: SSSD-Extract: Dumping AD Hashes and Domain Info from Linux Systems
item.description: How to extract cached Active Directory credentials, group memberships, user accounts and machine accounts from SSSD on domain-joined Linux systems. Includes extended fork with domain enumeration added during OSEP preparation.

item.title: HTB Machines for OSEP/PEN-300 Preparation: Complete Attack Chain Mapping
item.description: Curated list of HTB machines mapped to every PEN-300 syllabus topic: client-side execution, AV evasion, AD exploitation, MSSQL abuse, lateral movement and more. Includes TJNull OSEP-like list.

item.title: Cryptography I: Advanced Block Cipher Modes and Padding
item.description: Deep dive into CBC/CTR implementation details, IV/nonce management, padding oracle attacks, and performance comparison of encryption modes.

item.title: Cryptography I: Block Ciphers and Modes of Operation
item.description: Block cipher theory, PRP/PRF security, ECB/CBC/CTR modes, IV construction, and semantic security under chosen-plaintext attack (CPA).

item.title: Cryptography I: Stream Ciphers, PRGs, and Semantic Security
item.description: Stream cipher security, pseudo-random generators, information-theoretic security, and practical attacks on weak PRGs. Stanford Cryptography I Week 1.
